qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default configuration allows unauthenticated MongoDB explain output disclosure
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a query condition depth bypass causing DoS via uncontrolled recursion. This finding describes a default-true allowPublicExplain option that exposes MongoDB query planner internals. The root cause (missing configuration default vs. missing depth validation before transform pipeline), vulnerable code, and impact (info disclosure vs. DoS) are all different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL introspection access control bypass via __type field
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a query condition depth bypass causing DoS via uncontrolled recursion in the REST query transformation pipeline. This finding describes an incomplete introspection check in the GraphQL layer that only blocks __schema but not __type. The root cause (missing depth validation vs. incomplete string match), vulnerable component (query transform pipeline vs. IntrospectionControlPlugin), and impact (DoS vs. schema disclosure) are all different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL Playground exposes master key in client-side HTML response
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a query condition depth bypass causing DoS via uncontrolled recursion. This finding describes the master key being embedded in the GraphQL Playground HTML response. The root cause (missing pre-validation depth check vs. hardcoded credential in HTML template), vulnerable code path, and impact (DoS vs. credential exposure leading to full compromise) are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a query condition nesting depth bypass in the parse-server query transformation pipeline (CWE-674, uncontrolled recursion) that causes permanent server hang (DoS). None of the three qualifying findings identify this root cause. The findings cover: (1) MongoDB explain output disclosure via default config, (2) GraphQL introspection bypass via incomplete __type check, and (3) master key exposure in Playground HTML. All are genuine vulnerabilities in parse-server but none involve deeply nested query conditions, recursive processing in the transform pipeline, or denial of service through query depth.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-33498 specifically describes deeply nested query conditions with logical operators causing uncontrolled recursion before the depth guard fires in the transformation pipeline.
No finding references the query condition depth validation, the transform pipeline recursion, or the REST query processing path described in the advisory.
All three findings describe distinct root causes in different components: database explain configuration, GraphQL introspection checking, and Playground HTML rendering.
